# Team News: France vs. Morocco injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Jonathan O'Shea| 08 Jul 2026 21:00, Last updated: 09 Jul 2026 08:59

© Iconsport / DeFodi Images

Favourites France will continue their World Cup 2026 journey on Thursday, when they meet Morocco for a quarter-final clash in Boston.

Les Bleus fought their way past Paraguay in the last 16, while the Atlas Lions roared past tournament co-hosts Canada.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up all the latest team news for both teams.

---

## FRANCE vs. MOROCCO

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/france/world-cup-2026/team-news/GAME%20PAGE%20URL%20HERE

## FRANCE

Out: None

Doubtful: Marcus Thuram(calf), Aurelien Tchouameni(thigh)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Maignan; Kounde, Upamecano, Saliba, Digne; Kone, Rabiot; Dembele, Olise, Barcola; Mbappe

## MOROCCO

Out: Ismael Saibari(hamstring)

Doubtful: Chadi Riad (knee)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Bounou; Hakimi, Diop, Riad, Mazraoui; El Aynaoui, Bouaddi; Diaz, Ounahi, El Khannouss; Rahimi

---

Written by
highlights: Out: None ; Doubtful: Marcus Thuram (calf), Aurelien Tchouameni (thigh) ; Sports Mole's predicted XI: Maignan; Kounde, Upamecano, Saliba, Digne; ...

# France vs. Morocco projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup quarterfinals game in Boston

Marios Hamboullas

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

France and Morocco meet in the quarterfinals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup with a place in the last four on the line at Gillette Stadium in Boston.

Didier Deschamps' side has continued its pursuit of a third World Cup title, while Morocco is aiming to build on its historic run by reaching another semifinal on the biggest stage.

Who will start for France? Will Morocco make any changes after its impressive Round of 16 victory? The Sporting News looks at the latest projected lineups and team news ahead of Thursday's quarterfinal clash.

Ismael Saibari is a major doubt as Morocco prepare to take on France in the World Cup quarter-final tonight.

The new Bayern Munich forward was forced off in the first half of the last-16 win over Canada with a muscular injury, denying him the chance to add to his three goals at World Cup 2026.

"I haven't met with the doctor yet," Morocco head coach Mohamed Wahbi said. "Saibari has pain in his thigh."

# France vs Morocco Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Quarterfinal

They met in the 2022 World Cup semifinal, France won 2-0, and Morocco went home having already done something no African nation had done before. Four years later, they are doing it again and this time the qualification is more complete. Morocco is not here as a romantic story. They are here as a team on a 34-

Story by

France vs Morocco – Predicted lineup and team news

France face Morocco at Boston Stadium on Thursday evening in the World Cup quarter-finals, aiming to reach the semi-finals for the third consecutive tournament.

Didier Deschamps' side have won all five of their matches without once falling behind. Morocco's unbeaten run of 34 matches and their capacity to hurt teams on the counter make them a genuine threat, even against a France side that has won every match at this tournament. Deschamps steps onto the touchline for his 25th World Cup match, equalling Helmut Schön's long-standing record.

## France team news

https://sports.yahoo.com/soccer/teams/france/

Aurélien Tchouaméni remains absent. The Real Madrid midfielder has not returned to training since sustaining an adductor injury before the Paraguay match and is not expected to feature.

Manu Koné keeps his place in the midfield double

# Team News: France vs. Morocco injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Jonathan O'Shea| 08 Jul 2026 21:00, Last updated: 09 Jul 2026 08:59

© Iconsport / DeFodi Images

Favourites France will continue their World Cup 2026 journey on Thursday, when they meet Morocco for a quarter-final clash in Boston.
